overview.about: The Eiffel Tower, designed by Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 World's Fair, stands 330 metres tall on the Champ de Mars in Paris. Initially criticised by Parisian artists, it has become the world's most visited paid monument with over 7 million visitors annually. Every evening it sparkles for 5 minutes every hour.

highlights.label: 7 Million Visitors per Year
highlights.desc: The world's most visited paid monument — more people have stood at the top than any other structure on Earth

highlights.label: Sparkling Light Show Every Night
highlights.desc: Every evening after dark, 20,000 lights turn the tower into a diamond — best viewed from Trocadéro at the hour

highlights.label: Built in Just 2 Years, 2 Months
highlights.desc: Assembled 1887–1889 using 18,038 individual iron pieces — the fastest construction of any major monument in history

highlights.label: Panoramic View of All Paris
highlights.desc: From the third level at 276m, you can see the entire city on a clear day — 80km in every direction

overview.about: The Louvre in Paris is the world's largest and most visited art museum with 35,000 works on display across 72,735 square metres. Built as a fortress in 1190, it became a royal palace and opened as a museum in 1793. It holds the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo and Winged Victory of Samothrace.

highlights.label: World's Largest Art Museum
highlights.desc: 35,000 works spanning 5,000 years — from ancient Mesopotamia to 19th-century French masterpieces in a single palace

highlights.label: Home of the Mona Lisa
highlights.desc: Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece draws 10 million people annually — arrive early or book a timed ticket to see it properly

highlights.label: I.M. Pei's Glass Pyramid
highlights.desc: The iconic 1989 steel-and-glass pyramid entrance is now one of Paris's most photographed modern landmarks

highlights.label: A Royal Palace Turned Museum
highlights.desc: A medieval fortress became a royal residence then the world's greatest museum — 800 years of history in one building

overview.about: Palace of Versailles in Versailles, FR embodies the French genius for blending grandeur with elegance. As one of France's most celebrated heritage landmark destinations, it draws visitors with its perfect combination of history, beauty and that unmistakable Gallic sense of style and cultural pride.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today

overview.about: Grand Chamonix Forest in Chamonix, FR embodies the French genius for blending grandeur with elegance. As one of France's most celebrated natural wonder destinations, it draws visitors with its perfect combination of history, beauty and that unmistakable Gallic sense of style and cultural pride.

highlights.label: Biodiversity Hotspot
highlights.desc: Home to rare and endemic species — flora and fauna found nowhere else on the planet within pristine ecosystems

highlights.label: World-Renowned Trekking
highlights.desc: Trails from leisurely nature walks to multi-day high-altitude expeditions with breathtaking panoramic rewards

highlights.label: Wildlife Encounters
highlights.desc: Spot iconic wildlife in their natural habitat — guided walks dramatically increase sighting rates of rare species

highlights.label: Dramatic Natural Landscapes
highlights.desc: Awe-inspiring vistas of mountains, gorges, waterfalls and geological formations sculpted over millions of years

overview.about: Northern Marne-la-Vallee Shopping District in Marne-la-Vallee, FR embodies the French genius for blending grandeur with elegance. As one of France's most celebrated city destination destinations, it draws visitors with its perfect combination of history, beauty and that unmistakable Gallic sense of style and cultural pride.

highlights.label: Iconic Skyline & Architecture
highlights.desc: A skyline recognised across the globe — towers, bridges and boulevards that define an entire era of human design

highlights.label: World-Class Arts & Culture
highlights.desc: World-class museums, galleries, theatres and street art that shape global trends in fashion, film and music

highlights.label: Legendary Cuisine & Markets
highlights.desc: A melting pot of culinary traditions — street food, fine dining and bustling markets all within easy reach

highlights.label: Vibrant Nightlife & Events
highlights.desc: Rooftop bars, jazz clubs, night markets and international festivals keep the city electric long after dark

overview.about: Southern Normandy Luxury Resort in Normandy, FR embodies the French genius for blending grandeur with elegance. As one of France's most celebrated destination destinations, it draws visitors with its perfect combination of history, beauty and that unmistakable Gallic sense of style and cultural pride.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today
